title: Reading Files
description: Learn how to read entire files or line by line.

slides.title: Reading Files in PHP
slides.content:
# Getting Data from Files

PHP provides several ways to read files. The simplest is `file_get_contents()` which reads the entire file into a string.

```php
$content = file_get_contents('file.txt');
echo $content;
```

If the file doesn't exist, it returns `false` and raises a warning.

slides.title: Using file_get_contents
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
$filename = 'data.txt';
$content = file_get_contents($filename);
if ($content === false) {
    echo "Failed to read file.";
} else {
    echo $content;
}
?>

slides.title: Other Reading Functions

quizData.question: Which function reads an entire file into a string?
quizData.explanation: file_get_contents() reads the whole file into a string.

quizData.question: What does `fgets($handle)` return when it reaches the end of file?
quizData.explanation: fgets() returns false on EOF or error.
